slides.title: Unique Collections 🎯
slides.content:
# Introducing Sets

**Sets** are unordered collections of **unique** items:

```python
colors = {"red", "green", "blue"}
nums = {1, 2, 2, 3, 3, 3}  # Becomes {1, 2, 3}
```

No duplicates allowed!

slides.title: Creating Sets 💻
slides.codeSnippet:
# With curly braces
fruits = {"apple", "banana", "cherry"}
print("Fruits:", fruits)

# Duplicates automatically removed
nums = {1, 2, 2, 3, 3, 3}
print("Unique nums:", nums)

# From a list
letters = set(["a", "b", "a", "c"])
print("Letters:", letters)

# Empty set (not {}!)
empty = set()
print("Empty:", empty)

quizData.question: What happens when you add a duplicate to a set?
quizData.explanation: Sets automatically ignore duplicates — each item appears only once!

slides.title: Set Operations 🔧
slides.content:
# Mathematical Set Operations

| Operation | Symbol | Method |
|-----------|--------|--------|
| Union | `|` | `union()` |
| Intersection | `&` | `intersection()` |
| Difference | `-` | `difference()` |
| Symmetric Diff | `^` | `symmetric_difference()` |

slides.title: Set Math 💻
slides.codeSnippet:
a = {1, 2, 3, 4}
b = {3, 4, 5, 6}

print("A:", a)
print("B:", b)
print("Union (A|B):", a | b)
print("Intersection (A&B):", a & b)
print("Difference (A-B):", a - b)
print("Symmetric (A^B):", a ^ b)

quizData.question: What does intersection (&) return?
quizData.explanation: Intersection returns only items that appear in BOTH sets!

slides.title: Modifying Sets 💻
slides.codeSnippet:
colors = {"red", "green"}
print("Start:", colors)

colors.add("blue")
print("After add:", colors)

colors.remove("red")  # Error if not found
print("After remove:", colors)

colors.discard("yellow")  # No error if not found
print("After discard:", colors)

colors.update(["orange", "purple"])
print("After update:", colors)

slides.title: Common Use Cases 📋
slides.content:
# When to Use Sets

* 🔍 **Remove duplicates** from a list
* ✅ **Fast membership testing** (`in` is O(1)!)
* 🔄 **Mathematical operations** (union, intersection)
* 📊 **Track unique items**

slides.title: Practical Example 💻
slides.codeSnippet:
# Remove duplicates
items = [1, 2, 2, 3, 3, 3, 4, 4, 4, 4]
unique = list(set(items))
print("Unique items:", unique)

# Find common elements
list1 = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
list2 = [4, 5, 6, 7, 8]
common = set(list1) & set(list2)
print("Common:", common)

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## Fast Membership Testing

```python
# List: O(n) - slow for large lists
if x in my_list: ...

# Set: O(1) - fast!
if x in my_set: ...
```

Convert to set for faster lookups!

quizData.question: How to create an empty set?
quizData.explanation: {} creates an empty DICT! Use set() for an empty set.
